privacy-s0-p: This site is a personal portfolio. It collects the minimum necessary to function and to understand how visitors arrive. Nothing is sold, nothing is shared with advertisers. Webfonts are served from this domain — no third-party tracking from the moment the page loads.
privacy-s1-h2: 1. Data collected directly by the site
privacy-s1-li1: Contact form submissions. When you submit the contact form, the data you provide (first name, last name, email, subject, message) is sent via the Resend email API to info@ibaifernandez.com. It is not stored on this site or in any database. Resend's privacy policy applies to its handling: resend.com/legal/privacy-policy.
privacy-s1-li2: Anti-spam telemetry. To detect bots, the contact form records a timestamp when the page is loaded and includes it in the submission. It also uses Cloudflare Turnstile, which may load a small token from challenges.cloudflare.com. Turnstile's data handling: developers.cloudflare.com/turnstile.
privacy-s1-li3: Language preference. If you toggle the language, your choice is saved in localStorage on your device under the key portfolio_language. It never leaves your browser.
privacy-s1-li4: Consent decision. If you respond to the cookie banner, your choice is saved in localStorage under the key portfolio_consent. It never leaves your browser.
privacy-s1-legal-basis: Legal basis (GDPR Art. 6 · Ley 21.719 Art. 12). Analytics runs only with your consent (Art. 6(1)(a)). The contact form is processed to answer your enquiry, on the basis of your consent and the legitimate interest in replying (Art. 6(1)(a)/(f)). Anti-spam telemetry relies on the legitimate interest in keeping the form secure (Art. 6(1)(f)).
privacy-s1-retention: Retention. Contact-form emails are kept in the info@ibaifernandez.com mailbox for up to 24 months and then deleted, unless an ongoing conversation requires otherwise. The anti-spam timestamp is used only during submission; the language and consent values stay in your browser until you clear them.
privacy-s2-accept-li2: Your IP is anonymized before processing (anonymize_ip: true).
privacy-s2-accept-li3: Ad personalization, ad storage, and ad user data remain denied.
privacy-s2-change: Change your mind at any time: use the "Cookie preferences" link in the page footer to re-open the banner and update your choice.
privacy-s2-decline: If you decline (or never respond): GA4 is loaded but never fires. No cookies are set. No data is sent.
privacy-s2-retention: Google Analytics data retention is set to 14 months at the property level; only aggregated, non-identifying reports may persist beyond that.
privacy-s2-p1: This site uses Google Analytics 4 (property G-T8FTTWBQS3) to understand how visitors arrive and what they read. Analytics is loaded with consent denied by default using Google Consent Mode v2. Nothing is sent to Google until you accept analytics via the consent banner.
privacy-s3-fonts: Webfonts (Roboto, Inter, Josefin Sans, Roboto Mono) are self-hosted from this domain. Google Fonts is not used. Individual dossier pages may load page-specific webfonts from their providers — disclosed at the top of each dossier page when applicable.
privacy-s3-li1: Netlify — hosting CDN and the contact serverless function. Standard server logs (IP, user-agent, requested path) are retained per Netlify's policy: netlify.com/privacy.
privacy-s3-li2: Resend — email delivery for contact form messages.
privacy-s3-li3: Cloudflare Turnstile — bot detection on the contact form.
privacy-s3-li4: Google Analytics 4 — see section 2. Loaded only after explicit consent.
privacy-s3-li5: YouTube — embedded only on the Elm St dossier page, in privacy-enhanced mode (youtube-nocookie.com). YouTube cookies fire only after you press play.
privacy-s4-dsr-h3: How to exercise your rights (Data Subject Request)
privacy-s4-dsr-p: Email info@ibaifernandez.com with subject "Data Subject Request" and describe what you want (access, correction, deletion, etc.). I will respond within 30 calendar days. No proof of identity is required for simple deletion requests; access/portability requests may ask you to confirm the email address used in the original submission. Deletion requests concerning another person’s data will be declined, or will require confirmation of the email address used in the original submission.
privacy-s4-intro: Depending on where you reside, you may have rights under one or more of the following frameworks:
privacy-s4-li1: European Union / EEA / UK — General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and UK GDPR.
privacy-s4-li2: Chile — Ley 21.719 sobre Protección de Datos Personales.
privacy-s4-li3: Brasil — Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD, Lei nº 13.709/2018).
privacy-s4-li4: México — Ley Federal de Protección de Datos Personales en Posesión de los Particulares (LFPDPPP).
privacy-s4-li5: Other jurisdictions — equivalent local data-protection law may apply.
privacy-s4-rights-intro: Under these frameworks you generally have the right to:
privacy-s4-rights-li1: Know what data is processed about you (this page).
privacy-s4-rights-li2: Request access to, correction of, or deletion of your contact form submissions.
privacy-s4-rights-li3: Withdraw consent for analytics at any time — use the "Cookie preferences" link in the footer.
privacy-s4-rights-li6: Lodge a complaint with your supervisory authority — for example the AEPD (Spain/EEA), the Agencia de Protección de Datos Personales (Chile), or the ANPD (Brazil).
privacy-s5-p: The data controller is Ibai Fernández, the site owner. Contact via info@ibaifernandez.com. There is no joint controller and no designated data protection officer (DPO): the site carries out no large-scale systematic monitoring and processes no special-category data, so a DPO is not required under GDPR Art. 37(1) or equivalent.
privacy-s6-p: Some processors (Resend, Netlify, Cloudflare, Google) are headquartered in the United States and may process data outside your country of residence. Google and Cloudflare self-certify under the EU–US Data Privacy Framework; Resend and Netlify rely on the European Commission's Standard Contractual Clauses (SCCs). A copy of the applicable safeguards is available on request via info@ibaifernandez.com.
privacy-s7-p: Material changes will be reflected in the "Last updated" date at the top of this page. The full version history is in this repository's git log.
privacy-s8-p: In compliance with Article 10 of the Spanish Information Society Services Act (LSSI-CE): Name: Antonio Ibai Fernández Gutiérrez · NIF: 74853234X · Postal address: C/ Juan de Ortega, s/n, 29190, Málaga, Spain · Contact: info@ibaifernandez.com. kanban-dossier-tagline: Multi-tenant Kanban for teams that work with clients. Separate what's yours, what belongs to the team, and what belongs to the client — and decide who sees what at any moment.
kanban-dossier-problem-intro: Off-the-shelf Kanban tools force you to choose between two worlds. Either you manage your personal work in one app, or you manage team projects in another. And once clients come in, multiply that by all the accounts and permissions you need to maintain.
kanban-dossier-problem-list-1: Mix everything in a single SaaS app — you lose control over the data, you're locked into the vendor, and clients see things they shouldn't.
kanban-dossier-problem-list-2: Run one tool per type of work — permanent friction, duplicated accounts, context lost between context switches.
kanban-dossier-problem-list-3: Self-host something generic open source — it works, but you lose the UX, the notifications, the email digest, and the client/team separation you actually need.
kanban-dossier-problem-outro: AGLAYA Kanban Desk doesn't force you to choose. Three types of workspace in a single UI, full data sovereignty, modern Kanban mechanics with accessible drag-and-drop.
kanban-dossier-system-p1: The product runs on a strict five-level hierarchy. Each level inherits the visibility restrictions of the level above, and isolation is guaranteed both in code and in the database.
kanban-dossier-system-p2: Three workspace types cover three worlds: personal for individual work, internal for team projects, external for spaces shared with clients. Clients only see the external workspaces explicitly assigned to them. The team sees everything internal. You see everything. Switching a workspace to external triggers an explicit warning — its visibility changes.
kanban-dossier-chain-5-sub: 37 Postgres policies, rejects if app layer fails
kanban-dossier-timeline-b-meta: 19 findings · 2 criticals mitigated in flight (XSS upload, missing backup)
kanban-dossier-timeline-c-meta: 18 findings · GDPR + Ley 21.719 + LGPD · 6 archived DPAs
kanban-dossier-timeline-d-meta: 18 findings · Sentry + CI workflow + daily R2 backup
kanban-dossier-system-p3: Isolation does not rely on a single layer. Every request goes through concentric middleware (requireAuth → requireRole → requireWorkspaceMember) on the Express API, and the database reinforces isolation with Row Level Security on Postgres using SECURITY DEFINER functions. Even if the application layer failed, the database would reject the query. The client never touches Supabase tables directly — it only uses supabase.auth.* for authentication; all data flows through the backend.
kanban-dossier-state-version: Current release. Daily production use by the AGLAYA team at kanban.aglaya.biz.
kanban-dossier-state-tests: Jest + Supertest, full-stack server + client suite (102 passing, 4 skipped pre-existing with documented reason).
kanban-dossier-state-mariana: Self-run four-front audit — UX, Security, Multi-jurisdictional Legal, DevOps. 38 findings remediated post-Sprint 1.
kanban-dossier-state-critical: Includes two criticals mitigated in flight during the audit itself (XSS via upload, missing backups). Every critical from audit close, now resolved.
kanban-dossier-state-rls: Row Level Security active with SECURITY DEFINER functions. 37 policies verified in backup-restore smoke test.
kanban-dossier-state-backup: 30-day retention. Green smoke test: 10 of 10 tables restored correctly.
kanban-dossier-state-dpas: Supabase (with TIA), Resend, Railway, Netlify, Cloudflare, Microsoft — GDPR Art. 28.
kanban-dossier-state-sentry: @sentry/node server-side with SENTRY_RELEASE tagged per deploy. Captures 5xx + attempted Netlify proxy bypass.
kanban-dossier-frontier-intro: What the product does not do or does not yet offer, made explicit:
kanban-dossier-frontier-not-1: Not multi-organization yet — intentionally single-tenant, Phase 5 with no fixed date (ADR-020).
kanban-dossier-frontier-not-2: No SSO and no MFA — classic JWT auth with refresh token, sufficient for current internal use.
kanban-dossier-frontier-not-3: No turn-key self-hosted version for third parties — MIT-licensed code and public repo, but it operates as a single instance at kanban.aglaya.biz.
kanban-dossier-frontier-not-4: Not debt-free — ~35 open + 3 partial findings in the post-Mariana Trench backlog (mostly medium and low), all catalogued.
kanban-dossier-frontier-outro: The boundary is not defensive marketing: it's an architectural decision. The product serves one concrete use case (AGLAYA + clients) and grows based on real usage evidence, not aspirational roadmap.
kanban-dossier-proves-1-body: Data isolation enforced at two layers: concentric Express middleware (requireAuth → requireRole → requireWorkspaceMember) and RLS on Postgres with SECURITY DEFINER functions. Even if the application layer fails, the database rejects the query. All 9 tables with active RLS, 37 policies verified in production smoke test, 17 foreign keys and 17 ON DELETE clauses for referential integrity.
kanban-dossier-proves-2-body: Fifteen ADRs document every non-obvious architectural decision (ADR-011 through ADR-025). Migration of cards.category from TEXT to UUID FK with ON DELETE SET NULL. Canonical separation of app.js (Express config) and index.js (TCP entry point). Seven performance indexes on high-frequency columns. State management deferred until real friction appears (ADR-025).
kanban-dossier-proves-3-body: Self-run Mariana Trench audit across four dimensions: UX and accessibility, security and database, multi-jurisdictional legal, DevOps and observability. 79 formal findings, 38 closed post-Sprint 1 (18 critical + 15 high + 5 medium), ~35 in catalogued backlog. Each finding carries ID, severity, evidence, mitigation and commit SHA. Two criticals mitigated in flight during the audit itself.
kanban-dossier-proves-4-body: Dedicated privacy policy published at /privacidad, separate from the corporate one. Self-delete (DELETE /api/auth/me) and self-export (GET /api/auth/me/export) endpoints — GDPR Art. 17 and 20 covered. Six DPAs archived in docs/legal/dpas/. Cross-coverage of GDPR (Europe) + Ley 21.719 (Chile) + LGPD (Brazil).
kanban-dossier-proves-5-body: Sentry initialised on the server (@sentry/node) with SENTRY_RELEASE tagged per deploy. Captures 5xx and attempted Netlify proxy bypass (B-03). CI workflow on GitHub Actions runs npm test on every PR. Healthcheck /api/health with rate-limit skip for uptime monitors. Daily backup on Cloudflare R2 via GitHub Actions cron, 30-day retention, green smoke test (10 of 10 tables restored).
kanban-dossier-proves-6-body: Sovereign infrastructure: Postgres on Supabase but data exportable via pg_dump at any moment. Backups on Cloudflare R2 (own account). Email via Resend but cron on GitHub Actions (not owned by the email provider). Frontend on Netlify, backend on Railway — both contractually portable. MIT-licensed code in a public repository.
kanban-dossier-detail-1-p1: Strict hierarchy: Organization → Workspace → Board → Column → Card. Five levels, each inherits restrictions from the one above. Workspaces are typed (personal / internal / external) and that type determines content visibility.
kanban-dossier-detail-1-p2: Five roles coexist in the system: superadmin (universal auditability), admin (macro management), workspace owner, workspace member, client (only assigned external workspaces). Each action has an explicit permission matrix documented in docs/PERMISSIONS.md.
kanban-dossier-detail-1-p3: Data isolation in two layers: concentric Express middleware (requireAuth → requireRole → requireWorkspaceMember) plus RLS on Postgres with SECURITY DEFINER policies. The client never touches Supabase tables directly — only the supabase.auth.* endpoint for authentication; all other data always flows through the backend.
kanban-dossier-detail-2-p1: Backend Node.js + Express 4 with canonical separation: app.js (configuration + routes + middlewares) and index.js (TCP entry point) — ADR-024. JWT with refresh token + 15-minute access (B-02 post-audit). Helmet with CSP in production. Global rate limiting + endpoint-specific limits on sensitive routes.
kanban-dossier-detail-2-p2: Frontend React 18 + Vite + TailwindCSS. @dnd-kit for drag-and-drop with KeyboardSensor (A-02 post-audit, WCAG 2.1.1). Built-in avatar crop. Deterministic decorative mini-kanban on workspace cards for visual identity without external assets.
kanban-dossier-detail-2-p3: Email via Resend. Admin digest + user digest sent daily. Server-side node-cron replaced with GitHub Actions cron (better SLA, no live process on the server, non-formal ADR — operational decision documented in INCIDENTS.md).
kanban-dossier-detail-2-p4: Supabase database: nine tables, all nine with active RLS post-audit (B-04 closed the organizations gap), 17 foreign keys, 37 policies verified in production smoke test, 7 performance indexes on high-frequency columns (workspace_members.user_id, notifications.user_id partial WHERE read=false, cards.board_id, columns.board_id, boards.workspace_id, users.organization_id).
kanban-dossier-detail-3-p1: Self-run audit across four phases between 2026-05-27 and 2026-05-28. Four dimensions: Phase A (UX, accessibility, performance, SEO), Phase B (security, database, architecture), Phase C (legal — GDPR, Ley 21.719 of Chile, LGPD of Brazil, DPAs, cookies, retention), Phase D (DevOps, observability, documentation, maintainability).
kanban-dossier-detail-3-p2: Outcome: 79 formal findings, 38 closed post-Sprint 1 (18 critical + 15 high + 5 medium), ~35 in catalogued backlog + 3 partials at closing. Each finding carries ID, dimension, severity, evidence, mitigation and commit SHA in findings.json.
kanban-dossier-detail-3-p3: Two criticals mitigated in flight during the audit itself:
kanban-dossier-detail-3-li1: B-CRIT-01 · CVSS 8.0: exploitable XSS via SVG upload. Multer with no fileFilter accepted arbitrary MIMEs, the Netlify /uploads/* proxy gave same-origin, and the JWT in localStorage was stealable. Four-layer mitigation: extension blocklist + MIME allowlist + magic-bytes validation via file-type@16.5.4 + five regression tests. Commit 402b0d7.
kanban-dossier-detail-3-li2: B-CRIT-02: missing backup on Supabase Free plan (no daily backups, no PITR). A single DROP or bad migration meant total data loss. Mitigation: daily GitHub Actions cron, pg_dump with PG17 client, upload to Cloudflare R2 via native API (R2 rejects cfut_ tokens on the S3-compat endpoint), 30-day retention, full runbook in docs/runbooks/db-restore.md. Final commit 3ae6541.
kanban-dossier-detail-3-p4: After closing, the 16 remaining open criticals from the audit were resolved in four Sprint 1 batches: 8 accessibility (labels, KeyboardSensor, role=dialog, focus-trap, skip-link, aria-labels), 5 legal (own privacy policy, docs/legal/, self-delete and self-export endpoints), 3 operational (Sentry, CI, SECURITY.md sync with post-audit reality). Added to the two criticals mitigated in flight: 18 criticals remediated in total.
kanban-dossier-detail-4-p1: Cross-coverage of three data protection frameworks. Decisions documented and formally declined where appropriate — full auditability of every decision.
kanban-dossier-detail-4-p2: GDPR (Europe): Art. 13/14 covered by a dedicated privacy policy published at /privacidad (separate from the corporate one at aglaya.biz). Art. 17 (erasure) covered by DELETE /api/auth/me. Art. 20 (portability) covered by GET /api/auth/me/export. Art. 28 (processors) covered by 6 archived DPAs in docs/legal/dpas/: Supabase with TIA, Resend, Railway, Netlify, Cloudflare, Microsoft.
kanban-dossier-detail-4-p3: Ley 21.719 (Chile): Art. 14 ter (information to the data subject) covered by the policy and the self-service endpoints. LGPD (Brazil): Art. 9 (information) and Art. 50 (governance) covered by policy + designated DPO (privacidad@aglaya.biz).
kanban-dossier-detail-4-p4: Formally declined decisions: external legal review (€500-1500) declined (policy approved in-house by the audit, re-evaluable if EU volume exceeds 5,000 unique users / year), EU Art. 27 representative not mandatory at current volume, trilingual policy version postponed until real traction justifies it.
kanban-dossier-detail-5-p1: Sentry initialised server-side (@sentry/node required BEFORE any other require in server/index.js). Environment variables SENTRY_DSN, SENTRY_ENVIRONMENT and SENTRY_RELEASE configured on Railway. The error middleware in server/app.js captures 5xx (Sentry.captureException) and attempted Netlify proxy bypass (B-03 monitor). Client-side Sentry not added yet.
kanban-dossier-detail-5-p2: Backup workflow on GitHub Actions: cron 17 3 * * * UTC, pg_dump with PG17 client, upload to Cloudflare R2 via native API (/accounts/{id}/r2/buckets/{b}/objects/{key} with bearer cfut_; R2 rejects those tokens on the S3-compat endpoint, which is why we had to migrate from rclone and aws-cli to boto3 and finally to the native API). 30-day automatic retention.
kanban-dossier-detail-5-p3: CI workflow on GitHub Actions runs npm test on every PR. Current state: 102 tests green / 4 skipped (pre-existing auth/admin/security with legacy mocks requiring a larger refactor; skip documented with reason). The 4 skipped are deliberate; any new red blocks merge.
kanban-dossier-detail-5-p4: Healthcheck /api/health with real dependency verification — returns non-200 if Supabase or critical secrets do not respond. Covered by a dedicated test.
kanban-dossier-detail-6-p1: ~35 open findings + 3 partials post-Sprint 1, all catalogued by ID, severity and effort in docs/backlog/audit-mariana-roadmap.md. Sprint 2 covers the remaining highs: leftover a11y, B-03 Railway custom domain + Cloudflare WAF, refactor of pre-existing legacy tests. Sprints 3+ cover medium and low.
kanban-dossier-detail-6-p2: Partial findings still pending 100% closure: A-20 (h1 added on LoginPage, missing on WorkspaceDashboard / AdminPage / Toolbar / ResetPasswordPage), B-03 (monitor active, missing Railway custom domain + Cloudflare WAF/Tunnel — runbook docs/runbooks/railway-custom-domain.md ready), C-09 (DPIA template created; formal version pending until user growth or an external client demands it).
kanban-dossier-detail-6-p3: Drift resolved: package.json declared version 1.1.5 while the production product declared v1.3.1. Fixed on 2026-06-03.

scanner-dossier-tagline: Preparatory RegTech for auditing the public surface of Chilean websites under the new Data Protection Act. Operated by AGLAYA.
scanner-dossier-pipeline-alt: Scanner pipeline: preflight eligibility filter, consensus engine over 5 independent passes, rules first LLM after, 90.2% completion on preflight-filtered domains (n=716), 100% traceable evidence (n=1562 scans with findings), and delivery of a preparatory audit with explicit legal boundary
scanner-dossier-problem-intro: Chilean companies need to audit their public exposure under the new Ley 21.719 without hiring expensive bespoke consulting or waiting three weeks for a human. Today the real options are four:
scanner-dossier-problem-list-1: Review everything manually — slow, costly in internal time, and error-prone.
scanner-dossier-problem-list-2: Don't review anything and face the fines when they arrive.
scanner-dossier-problem-list-3: Pay lawyers, generally by the hour and at rates few SMEs can afford.
scanner-dossier-problem-list-4: Use generic tools that don't understand the Chilean regulatory framework and don't produce traceable evidence.
scanner-dossier-system-p1: The product receives a public URL. Before running the full scan, a preflight module automatically verifies that the domain is reachable from Chile, that it isn't blocking automation, that it resolves to a valid public IP, and that it carries web content relevant under Ley 21.719. If it doesn't pass, the client is notified immediately at no cost. If it does, the real engine kicks in.
scanner-dossier-system-p2: The scan engine runs five independent consensus passes over the same domain in isolated Playwright contexts, orchestrated from the RQ worker's async loop. Each pass visits the site from scratch, captures the full HTML of the main page and policy pages, detects cookies emitted before and after interaction, and identifies forms, consent banners, and any external resource loads. That capture is analysed by the deterministic regulatory rules engine: 12 versioned controls in matrix_v1.json (with control_id, base_legal, obligacion, severidad_inicial) over the relevant Ley 21.719 exposure vectors.
scanner-dossier-system-p3: With all five analyses complete, the consensus engine compares results run by run. Only alerts that appear in four or five of the five executions make it into the report. Anything below that threshold is dropped — network variability, dynamic content, or timing artefacts stay out. No consensus, no alert.
scanner-dossier-system-p4: The rules engine is composed of two separate services: Rules Engine (interprets each capture against the regulatory catalogue) and Control Matrix (maps findings to auditable controls). Both consume Matriz Normativa v1 — the catalogue of deterministic checks maintained as versioned data, not embedded code. The same model drives the scoring and semantic layer of each alert.
scanner-dossier-system-p5: AI doesn't decide what an alert is. The deterministic regulatory rules do — traceable, auditable, repeatable. Claude steps in only at the end, to compile collected evidence and write the report in plain language. Each alert is anchored to an HTML fragment captured from the real site, serialised against the versioned contract scan_v1.schema.json and archived as scan_v1.json. The final report conforms to report_v1.schema.json. Schema-first, not improvisation. The operator can read, cite or dispute every evidence node.

scanner-dossier-proves-2-title: From nightly panel to product in weeks
scanner-dossier-proves-2-body-1: The project started as a nightly research panel: 1,000 domains being scanned every night to tune the rules engine. The inflection point was a non-obvious architectural decision — compressing that validation loop into running 5 independent passes per domain on each individual audit, with a consensus engine on top of the results.
scanner-dossier-proves-2-body-2: What could have been months of iteration to reach statistical robustness became weeks of development — and a commercially launchable product with stats stronger than any nightly run.
scanner-dossier-proves-3-body: "Rules first, LLM after" — traceability beats generation. Single main branch — speed beats ceremonious workflow. Consensus over 5 runs per scan — confidence beats raw speed.
scanner-dossier-proves-4-title: Security as a layer, not afterthought
scanner-dossier-proves-4-body: Full external security audit: all critical, high, medium, and low findings closed before launch, plus five findings detected after opening to external traffic. SSRF hardening with denylist + IDN normalization + redirect re-verification. Redis rate limiting with atomic Lua script (correct multi-worker). Atomicity as a pattern: three critical operations (claim_webhook_event, save_initial_record, validate_and_use) implemented as atomic with PK constraints or Lua scripts. IP allowlist on Lemon Squeezy webhooks with dedicated test. Complete HTTP headers (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, Permissions-Policy).
scanner-dossier-proves-5-body: Ley 21.719 domain + product + go-to-market. Not a PoC: documented Obviously Awesome positioning (score 6.9/10), StoryBrand messaging applied, Jobs-To-Be-Done formalised, current pricing and commercial offer separated as documents, competitive analysis named (C-ONLINE) and category/adjacency references. Controlled public funnel landing → preflight → checkout → consensus → delivery. Versioned privacy policy with changelog (v1.2 → v1.3) — compliance treated as product, not afterthought.
scanner-dossier-proves-6-body: Product + commercial landing + agency landing + public documentation + technical blog + Meta Ads (Pixel + CAPI server-side) + promotional video with AI avatar + Lemon Squeezy checkout + editorial delivery + commercial booking (Cal.com with own webhook) + support. One person, one repo, one main. AGLAYA as commercial operator.
scanner-dossier-proves-7-body: Versioned JSON Schema contracts (scan_v1.schema.json, report_v1.schema.json) as the formal interface between capture, consensus engine, rules engine, and report generation. System critical invariants listed in operational documentation. Post-deploy protocol formalised per release. Postmortems documented per case. Not code that works — code that can be defended.
scanner-dossier-proves-8-title: D+7 feedback loop with real clients
scanner-dossier-proves-8-body: Active social-proof plan: feedback panel 7 days after delivery (PR37), cross-check System → CRM on emitted audits, engine iteration with real field data. Not build-and-pray.
scanner-dossier-detail-1-p1: The client URL enters through the public landing. The first step is always the preflight: the system automatically checks that the domain exists, is reachable from Chile, isn't blocking automation, and carries web content relevant under Ley 21.719. If the domain doesn't pass, the client receives immediate notification at no cost.
scanner-dossier-detail-1-p2: If it passes, contact email is requested and the scan engine starts. The RQ worker runs five consensus passes over the domain, each in its own isolated Playwright context inside the worker's async loop. Each pass captures the visible public layer: HTML, cookies emitted, forms, consent banners, published policies, external resources loaded. The capture is analysed by the deterministic regulatory rules engine: 12 versioned controls in matrix_v1.json, each with control_id, base_legal, obligacion, and severidad_inicial. A semantic LLM layer runs on top of deterministic findings to enrich evidence and write.
scanner-dossier-detail-1-p3: With all five analyses complete, the consensus engine compares results. Only alerts appearing in four or five of the five executions are consolidated. Anything below that threshold is discarded — network variability, dynamic content, cookies under race conditions, timing artefacts.
scanner-dossier-detail-1-p4: Once consensus is reached, the client gets the Lemon Squeezy checkout link. Payment triggers report generation: the consolidated evidence in scan_v1.json goes to Claude, which transforms it into an editorial PDF report with AGLAYA branding, traceable audit ID, date, and engine version. The report arrives by email in minutes.
scanner-dossier-detail-1-p5: Exceptional case: if consensus isn't reached — fewer than four of five workers agree on a result — the case enters manual review before any invoice or report is issued.
scanner-dossier-detail-2-p1: Backend Python + FastAPI; separate routers for admin, audits, payments, feedback, contact, crm, health. Worker RQ isolated in a separate process from the API (via backend/worker.py) with Sentry initialised at startup. Playwright runs in the worker's async loop, with _lock = Lock() per-process documented as a critical invariant.
scanner-dossier-detail-2-p2: Job queue Redis + RQ; consensus job results are cached in Redis with 90-day TTL — surviving restarts and redeploys. SQLite for operational data (checkout, payment, delivery), with explicit guardrail (_warn_if_sqlite_with_multi_worker) that warns if config isn't scale-compatible.
scanner-dossier-detail-2-p3: /health endpoint with real dependency check: returns HTTP 503 if DB or Redis go down, not a cosmetic 200.
scanner-dossier-detail-2-p4: Frontend plain HTML/JS on Netlify edge. No JS framework — the capture form is 50 lines, doesn't need React. Consent Mode v2 loaded before GA4. Cookies only if the user consents. Cloudflare Turnstile for frictionless bot detection.
scanner-dossier-detail-2-p5: Payments via Lemon Squeezy with replay-protected webhook: claim_webhook_event(event_key) in backend/app/services/webhook_security.py returns False if the event was already processed (idempotency via PK constraint). The system never charges twice. IP allowlist on the Lemon Squeezy webhook, verified by test.
scanner-dossier-detail-2-p6: Server-side integrations: Meta CAPI (meta_capi.py:send_event) for Ads conversions without depending on the client pixel; Cal.com (cal_bookings.py + cal_webhook) for commercial booking with its own idempotent webhook.

SSRF hardening (private-IP denylist, IDN/punycode normalisation, redirect re-verification, hostname variants). RedisRateLimiter with atomic Lua script replaces InMemoryRateLimiter — correct rate limiting in multi-worker. Real healthcheck /health validates DB + Redis and returns HTTP 503 if any critical dependency fails.

crm-dossier-tagline: Proprietary operational CRM, extracted from the legal-reg-tech monorepo. Lead capture, deal pipeline, and full interaction traceability. Zero SQL injection, zero SSRF, zero RCE — verified in Mariana Trench audit.
crm-dossier-problem-p1: When you sell compliance audits to businesses, each lead is a potential B2B client whose identity and negotiation status cannot be stored in a third-party CRM. Regulatory pressure is the product: exposing it to an external SaaS is a structural contradiction.
crm-dossier-problem-list-1: External SaaS CRM — your leads live on someone else's servers, with no control over retention or portability.
crm-dossier-problem-list-2: Shared spreadsheet — no typed pipeline, no scoring, no interaction traceability, no email integration.
crm-dossier-problem-list-3: Generic open-source CRM — works but requires customisation and security guarantees that no generic product delivers aligned to your stack.
crm-dossier-problem-outro: CRM AGLAYA solves this with full data sovereignty: own Postgres, typed pipeline, dual scoring, append-only activity log, and complete traceability — no intermediate vendor.
crm-dossier-system-p1: The deal pipeline follows six typed stages. Each stage has precise semantics: leads enter as New, advance through contact and session, reach proposal, and close as active, won, or lost. No free-form states.
crm-dossier-system-p2: Each contact has two independent scoring columns: scanner_score (0–50) measures regulatory compliance of the public surface; lead_score (0–100) measures commercial potential. Orthogonal dimensions — high regulatory score does not imply high purchase potential.
crm-dossier-system-p3: The admin panel auth uses constant-time comparison (safe_constant_time_compare() in auth_utils.py) that correctly handles None and non-ASCII characters without raising exceptions. The roster loop has no break — uniform execution time regardless of result, preventing user enumeration via timing. 100% ORM data access — no raw SQL strings anywhere in the repository.

crm-dossier-proves-2-body: safe_constant_time_compare() in auth_utils.py: correctly handles None, empty strings, and non-ASCII characters without raising exceptions. Roster loop with no break — uniform execution time, eliminating timing side-channel for user enumeration. Clean pip-audit: zero CVEs in the dependency tree.
crm-dossier-proves-3-body: 100% data access via SQLAlchemy ORM — no raw SQL strings anywhere in the repository. HMAC Svix v1 on Resend webhook, correct verification, fails closed: 503 if secret not configured, 401 if signature incorrect. Pydantic on lead capture with email shape, max-length, and score range validation.
crm-dossier-proves-4-body: Operational ARCO endpoints: full JSON export and erasure walk with row counts. Legal baseline with processor inventory, legal basis per activity, breach runbook, data classification, and DPIA decision. Cross-coverage Ley 21.719 (Chile) + GDPR (Europe) + LGPD (Brazil).
crm-dossier-proves-5-body: 61 formal findings across four fronts (UX, Security, Legal, DevOps). 28 findings mitigated during the audit cycle (9 critical + 13 high + 6 medium), 33 in catalogued backlog. Each finding carries ID, severity, evidence, mitigation, and commit SHA in findings.json.
crm-dossier-proves-6-body: Two orthogonal scoring dimensions: scanner_score (regulatory compliance 0–50) and lead_score (commercial potential 0–100). Six typed deal stages with precise semantics. Append-only activity log guarantees full traceability. Optimistic concurrency with expected_updated_at token to prevent simultaneous edits.
crm-dossier-detail-1-p1: Three main entities: Contact (captured leads), Deal (commercial opportunities linked to a contact), Activity (append-only interaction log — email opened, session scheduled, proposal sent, note). The activity log is never edited or deleted: only appended.
crm-dossier-detail-1-p2: Two scoring columns per contact: scanner_score (0–50) captures the Scanner Ley 21.719 result on the lead's public surface; lead_score (0–100) captures commercial potential by operator criteria. Independent dimensions because regulatory compliance and purchase potential do not always correlate.
crm-dossier-detail-1-p3: Active foreign keys on contact_id and deal_id with precise semantic cascade: deleting a deal cascades to its activities; deleting a contact is restricted while it has associated deals. Partial UNIQUE INDEX on audit_id WHERE NOT NULL — prevents duplicate linked audits without affecting contacts with no audit. Schema defined in database.py.
crm-dossier-detail-2-p1: Backend Python · FastAPI with SQLAlchemy ORM. Database: PostgreSQL on Railway (SQLite in development). No raw SQL strings at any point — 100% ORM access. Capture payload validation with Pydantic: email shape, max-length, and score range.
crm-dossier-detail-2-p2: Resend webhook with correct HMAC Svix v1 verification. Idempotency guaranteed by processed events table + claim_webhook_event method in crm.py — email provider retries do not duplicate activity rows. Optimistic concurrency via expected_updated_at token.
crm-dossier-detail-2-p3: Observability: Sentry SDK initialised. Structured JSON logs. Email PII masked in logs. Rate limiting on exposed endpoints. Complete HTTP security headers: HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, Permissions-Policy. TrustedHost middleware. Meta robots noindex,nofollow on the admin panel.
crm-dossier-detail-2-p4: Daily backup: Railway cron fires pg_dump → Cloudflare R2. Restore runbook documented. Dependabot active. CI with pytest + ruff on each PR. pre-commit configured.

outreach-dossier-tagline: B2B campaign intelligence SaaS. From a raw contact list to an approved, push-ready strategy — with hypothesis-first LLM onboarding, deterministic scoring, AI-generated copy, and push to 4 ESPs.
outreach-dossier-problem-intro: Building a professional segmentation analysis for an email marketing client — with scoring, tiers, personalised copy per segment, and a campaign strategy — takes days. Every time. From scratch. No reuse. Inconsistent quality across projects. There are three classic paths, and all three are bad:
outreach-dossier-problem-list-1: Do it manually — days of artisan work, undocumented criteria, non-reproducible results, impossible to scale beyond two simultaneous clients.
outreach-dossier-problem-list-2: Use generic email marketing tools — they manage sending but not intelligence: no scoring, no strategic segmentation, no tier-differentiated copy, no client context.
outreach-dossier-problem-list-3: Outsource to agencies for each campaign — expensive, slow, the knowledge stays outside, the client never owns their own strategy.
outreach-dossier-problem-outro: AGLAYA Outreach turns that artisan process into a parametrised platform. The second client costs technically a tenth of the first. The strategy lives in the system, not in the consultant's head.
outreach-dossier-system-p1: The product operates on a five-stage pipeline that transforms raw data into an approved campaign. Each stage has explicit guarantees: no malformed data passes from Cleaning to Normalisation, no copy reaches the client without going through the automated reviewer.
outreach-dossier-system-p2: Three fully decoupled layers ensure each component evolves without breaking the others: Intelligence (data quality, Apollo enrichment, hypothesis-first LLM onboarding) → Strategy (deterministic YAML DSL scoring, tiers A/B/C/D, planner) → Campaigns (GPT-4o-mini copywriter, standalone HTML showcase, A4 PDF, ESP push). Input adapters (CSV, Brevo API, MailerLite API) and output adapters (HTML, PDF, ESP push) are interchangeable — the core has no knowledge of providers.
outreach-dossier-chain-2-sub: dedup · BOM/latin-1 encoding fix · MX validation
outreach-dossier-chain-4-sub: deterministic YAML DSL · same input → same tier, always
outreach-dossier-chain-5-sub: copy never reaches the client without double validation
outreach-dossier-system-p3: The scoring is completely deterministic: the same data plus the same config always produces the same tiers. This makes the real bill-capital dataset work as a canonical regression fixture — any change to the engine is verified immediately. Copy generated by GPT-4o-mini never reaches the client without going through reviewer.py (automatic validation: subject length, spam words, tier coherence) and an explicit two-step human approval: first segmentation, then copy.

outreach-dossier-state-db: Row Level Security active on all tables. Explicit GRANTs per table. TTL on welcome_sessions (30d) and llm_calls retention (90d).

outreach-dossier-state-scoring: YAML DSL with keyword_match, regex_match, set_lookup, numeric_range rules. Same CSV + same config → same tiers, always. Python plugin support for custom logic.
outreach-dossier-state-onboarding: The LLM fetches the client's website (Jina AI fallback for SPAs), builds business hypotheses and presents them for validation. The million-dollar client doesn't fill out forms.
outreach-dossier-frontier-intro: What the product does not do or does not yet offer, explicit:
outreach-dossier-frontier-not-1: No HubSpot adapter — fifth ESP planned but not implemented. The architecture supports it: one new file, zero core changes.
outreach-dossier-frontier-not-2: Not in active commercial launch — the product is in beta, operational and tested. Client acquisition is the next step, not a technical problem.
outreach-dossier-frontier-not-3: No No-Code panel — the operator configures in YAML/Supabase. The web UI is for the end client, not for configuring the engine. On the roadmap, not urgent technical backlog.
outreach-dossier-frontier-not-4: Not debt-free — 7 won't-fix findings (intentional design decisions), 3 deferred (density refactors: client.html, client_runs.py, inline handlers). Catalogued, not ignored.
outreach-dossier-frontier-outro: The frontier is not defensive marketing: it is intentional design. The product serves a concrete use case — agencies that need to convert contact lists into professional campaigns — and grows based on real usage evidence.
outreach-dossier-proves-1-body: The onboarding does not ask "what is your business?". It fetches the client's website (with Jina AI fallback for SPAs), builds structured hypotheses about pain points and objectives, and presents them for validation. The LLM arrives knowing things — the client confirms or redirects. This design decision — propose instead of interrogate — is the difference between a chatbot and a consultant. It reflects the "Million-Dollar Client" methodology (D-012): the system must feel like a senior strategy team, not a tool.
outreach-dossier-proves-2-body: Flask architecture refactored into 8 independent blueprints. Per-client config in Supabase (client_configs), with filesystem fallback for legacy clients. RLS active on all 13 tables — isolation does not rest on application-layer filters but on Postgres policies. Single-use, scoped magic links per workspace (H19). 14 migrations with explicit GRANTs on every CREATE TABLE — without the grant pattern, PostgREST returns a silent 42501 error.
outreach-dossier-proves-3-title: Adapter Pattern at scale — 4 ESPs, 1 interface
outreach-dossier-proves-3-body: Brevo, MailerLite, Mailchimp and MailJet are all unified under a single call: esp_adapter.push_contacts(contacts, config). The adapter detects the active ESP from the client config, normalises the payload, manages provider-specific authentication. Input adapters (CSV cleaner, Brevo API, MailerLite API) all feed into the same normalised TypedDict schema. Adding a fifth ESP requires exactly one new file — zero core changes, zero regressions.
outreach-dossier-proves-4-body: Mariana audit completed: 99 findings, 89 mitigated (90%), 100% of CRITICALs closed. CI pipeline on every push: gitleaks (secret scanning), pip-audit (CVEs in dependencies), Trivy (container scanning). TTL on welcome_sessions (30d) and llm_calls retention (90d) — no indefinite orphan data. Weekly purge cron via GitHub Actions (scripts/purge_old_data.py).
outreach-dossier-proves-5-title: GDPR as a product feature, not a checkbox
outreach-dossier-proves-5-body: Art.20 (portability): self-service ZIP export with config + runs + showcase comments, accessible from the client's own workspace. Art.17 (erasure): erasure request with a 14-day grace period, erasure_requests table in Supabase, full flow without manual operator intervention. Compliance is part of the product, not a patch added post-audit.
outreach-dossier-proves-6-title: Turning consulting into a scalable product
outreach-dossier-proves-6-body: AGLAYA Outreach was generalised from bill-capital-campaign, a real consulting engagement for Bill Capital Chile (2026). The parametrisation is complete: YAML scoring is deterministic (same CSV + config → same tiers, always), which makes the real bill-capital dataset a canonical regression fixture. The frame extractor (H17b) closes the learning loop: campaigns with open rate ≥35% or click rate ≥5% are converted into copyframes.yaml — the system learns from its own successes. The second client costs technically one tenth of the first.
outreach-dossier-detail-1-p1: The system operates on three fully decoupled layers. Intelligence: quality.py (B2B/B2C detection, field coverage, duplicates, MX validation), data_analyst.py (statistical analysis prior to LLM), intel_analyst.py + strategy_generator.py (LLM intelligence and strategy on segments), apollo_adapter.py (B2B enrichment). Strategy: score.py (YAML DSL engine + Python plugin), planner.py (deterministic campaign plan). Campaigns: copywriter.py (GPT-4o-mini), reviewer.py (automatic validation), html_showcase.py + pdf_report.py + esp_adapter.py.
outreach-dossier-detail-1-p2: Input adapters: csv_adapter.py + cleaner.py (XLSX/CSV → dedup → BOM/latin-1 encoding fix → syntax validation → MX domain verification) + brevo_adapter.py + mailerlite_adapter.py. All feed the same TypedDict schema (core/schema.py) that acts as an explicit contract between layers. Interchangeable output adapters with no core changes.
outreach-dossier-detail-1-p3: The Flask application is refactored into 8 blueprints registered in app.py (~1,228 LOC): auth, client, client_runs, dashboard, seo, showcase, webhooks, welcome. Independent services layer (services/): copy_service.py, copy_types.py, esp_service.py.
outreach-dossier-detail-2-p1: Backend Python 3 · Flask 3.0+ · Gunicorn 21+. No forced virtualenv — /usr/bin/python3 from the system for maximum iterative portability. 3 workers in production (GUNICORN_WORKERS=3 on Railway), 300s timeout for 60-120s LLM operations. NIXPACKS builder on Railway, auto-deploy on push to main.
outreach-dossier-detail-2-p2: Database Supabase PostgreSQL (project in São Paulo): 13 tables, 14 migrations, RLS active on all. Critical pattern: every migration with CREATE TABLE includes GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE ON public.<table> TO service_role — without the grant, PostgREST returns a silent 42501 error. Per-client config in Supabase (client_configs), filesystem fallback for legacy clients.
outreach-dossier-detail-2-p3: Auth: single-use, expiring magic links scoped to the client workspace. auth_tokens table + users table with superadmin / operator / client roles. No passwords. The client session lives in welcome_sessions (TTL 30d, expires_at field + lazy cleanup on each load).
outreach-dossier-detail-2-p4: AI: GPT-4o-mini for per-tier copywriting (subject, preheader, body, CTA) and conversational onboarding. Each call logged in llm_calls table (model, tokens, latency_ms, cost_usd) with 90-day retention. Jina AI as fallback for web extraction in SPAs (layer 2 of web_fetcher.py, no Playwright). Apollo.io People Match API for B2B enrichment with post-enrichment re-scoring.
outreach-dossier-detail-3-summary: LLM pipeline — onboarding, copywriter, reviewer, frame extractor
outreach-dossier-detail-3-p1: The hypothesis-first onboarding (welcome_llm.py, WS-2/3) uses SYSTEM_PROMPT_DATA_FIRST: the LLM receives the CSV analysis ({data_analysis_block}) and the client's web content ({web_context_block}) before the first question. Turn 1: A/B/C/D objective options. Turn 2: pain point hypotheses — presented for validation, not asserted without confirmation. Turn 3: sender. temperature=0.6 for conversational naturalness. The strategy.notes field captures all extra context the client adds.
outreach-dossier-detail-3-p2: The copywriter (copywriter.py) generates copy per tier (A/B/C/D) and per sequence email (subject, preheader, HTML body, CTA). The reviewer (reviewer.py) validates before presenting: subject length (40-60 chars), absence of spam words, tier coherence. The showcase bridge (showcase_bridge.py) converts the plan into the sequences format for the standalone HTML showcase.
outreach-dossier-detail-3-p3: The frame extractor (H17b, frame_extractor.py) closes the learning loop: it analyses campaign results with open rate ≥35% or click rate ≥5%, extracts structural elements from successful copy (hook, value proposition, CTA) and appends to copyframes.yaml. Never overwrites — only appends. Validated frames (validated: true) feed back into the copywriter in subsequent campaigns. The system improves with every campaign.
outreach-dossier-detail-4-p1: esp_adapter.push_contacts(contacts, config) detects the active ESP by config priority: brevo.list_id → mailerlite.output_group_id → mailchimp.list_id → mailjet.list_id. Each provider's auth logic and payload normalisation is encapsulated in its adapter — the core never sees provider differences.
outreach-dossier-detail-4-p2: Engagement webhooks (opened, clicked, unsubscribed, soft_bounce, hard_bounce, spam, delivered) normalised to the same canonical event_types in the campaign_events table, regardless of provider. Optional authentication via ?token=WEBHOOK_SECRET. Mailchimp requires a 200 response to the GET handshake — covered in blueprints/webhooks.py.
outreach-dossier-detail-4-p3: Async push via esp_service.trigger_push_async() to avoid blocking the request. The engagement history feeds the results view (H17): funnel by stage (delivered → opened → clicks → conversions), per-contact attribution, real rates by tier. The operator sees which tier converts best, which emails work, which contacts engage — in the same platform that generated the campaign.
outreach-dossier-detail-5-p1: Own four-front audit completed May-June 2026. 99 formal findings, 89 mitigated (90%), 100% of CRITICALs closed. 7 won't-fix (proportional to risk, documented decisions), 3 deferred (non-urgent density refactors: client.html 2000+ LOC, client_runs.py 1276 LOC, inline event handlers).

outreach-dossier-detail-5-p2: Key Batch 1 mitigations: migration 011 (security hardening: RLS + CHECK role + FK auth_tokens), migration 012 (welcome_sessions TTL + lazy cleanup), migration 013 (llm_calls retention + created_at index), migration 014 (GDPR erasure_requests), Gunicorn workers fixed to 3 (GUNICORN_WORKERS=3), NoOpLimiter fail-fast in production (B-HIGH-07), Mailchimp webhook anomaly logging (B-HIGH-08), base.html preconnect + theme-color + meta keywords (A-MED-06, A-LOW-03, C-LOW-04).
outreach-dossier-detail-6-summary: GDPR, data retention and honest backlog
outreach-dossier-detail-6-p1: Art.20 (portability): endpoint GET /client/<slug>/data-export generates a ZIP with client config + pipeline_runs + showcase comments. Accessible from the client's workspace, no operator intervention. Art.17 (erasure): endpoint POST /client/<slug>/request-erasure creates an entry in the erasure_requests table with scheduled_for = now() + 14 days. 14-day grace period before full workspace erasure.
outreach-dossier-detail-6-p2: Active retention policy: llm_calls purged after 90 days (migration 013 + weekly cron). campaign_events purged after 365 days. The cron runs via GitHub Actions (scripts/purge_old_data.py) — no live process on the server, better SLA.
outreach-dossier-detail-6-p3: Honest backlog: 7 won't-fix (intentional design — e.g., no forced HTTPS in local dev, no rate limiting on internal routes), 3 deferred (code density refactors that do not affect functionality or security). H18b (onboarding as premium feature) and the HubSpot adapter are the next two identified development blocks.
